items.briefing:
AI is making phishing attacks more convincing and harder to spot, with scammers now running highly organised, professional operations targeting everyday users including those in Australia. Separately, a Chinese cybercrime group known as TA4922 is expanding its attacks globally, meaning Australian organisations should be on alert. If your organisation uses SolarWinds Serv-U file transfer software, a newly exploited vulnerability is actively crashing servers — check with your IT team about applying patches immediately.

On a positive note, Google is rolling out fake call detection on Android phones, which can help staff identify scam calls. **Practical tip:** If you receive an unexpected email or call asking you to click a link or share login details, verify the request through a separate, trusted channel before acting.

featured.title: Google rolls out fake call detection on Android phones
featured.quote: Android users now get spoofed call warnings, addressing a widespread threat affecting millions of Australians daily.
